description:
## Background

Beadsmith can generate canonical Issue Location links but cannot yet receive them from the operating system. Tauri’s official Deep Link plugin provides startup and running delivery; Linux requires official Single Instance integration to route a second launch to the existing process. Startup delivery must wait until remembered Workspace restoration settles before deciding how to navigate.

Triage: `ready-for-agent`.

## What to build

Make canonical links targeting the exact Current Workspace open through the real desktop application on macOS and Linux. Handle both startup and already-running delivery, focus and reuse the existing window, integrate with Issue Navigation history and transient Settings, and show a dismissible global banner for malformed links.

## Technical Approach

- Add and configure Tauri’s official Deep Link plugin with the statically registered `beadsmithy` desktop scheme and required capability permissions.
- Add official Single Instance integration in the required registration order for Linux and focus/unminimize the existing main window when a second launch delivers a link. Retain macOS’s native running-app delivery path.
- Include Windows only if the same shared configuration works without Windows-specific engineering.
- Register the renderer listener early enough not to miss running events and read startup URLs through the plugin startup API.
- Treat a delivery array as last-URL-wins. Do not introduce a queue.
- Delay startup link resolution until initial remembered Workspace restoration and Issue Explorer loading have settled.
- Parse all received URLs through the shared Issue Location URI contract.
- For this slice, open links whose decoded Workspace path exactly matches the canonical Current Workspace path. Canonical aliases and other Workspace paths are handled in the next child.
- A startup link replaces the initial empty Issue Navigation Entry. A link received while running pushes one entry, so Back restores the prior destination.
- Reveal a target hidden by view/search using All Issues and cleared search. Preserve the prior context through Back.
- If Settings is open, restore its underlying Issue destination first, then push the running deep link so Back skips Settings.
- Keep a missing target routed and render Issue-not-found.
- Show malformed/unsupported links in a dismissible global `Could not open link` banner. Keep it until dismissed or a later deep link succeeds.
- Add the highest feasible real-desktop acceptance path for scheme delivery on macOS and Linux using disposable Workspaces and isolated application state.

## Considerations

- Installed desktop scheme behavior cannot be proven in jsdom. Package/build verification and a real process boundary are required.
- Static scheme registration is authoritative for packaged apps. Linux debug tests may use Tauri’s documented runtime registration mechanism without changing release semantics.
- The existing application product name and bundle identifier remain unchanged; only the custom scheme is `beadsmithy`.
- This ticket must not mutate the Workspace Catalog or prompt for a different path.
- Ensure listener cleanup remains correct under React development lifecycle behavior.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Packaged/configured macOS and Linux builds register the `beadsmithy` scheme.
- [ ] A startup link to the Current Workspace waits for restoration, replaces the initial entry, and opens the target Issue.
- [ ] A running link to the Current Workspace focuses/reuses the existing window and pushes one entry.
- [ ] Back after a running link restores the prior Issue destination.
- [ ] Deep linking while Settings is open closes Settings and Back skips Settings.
- [ ] Hidden and missing target Issues use the agreed reveal and Issue-not-found behavior.
- [ ] Malformed or unsupported links show a dismissible global error banner without changing Workspace or route.
- [ ] A later successful link clears the banner.
- [ ] Multiple delivered URLs use only the last URL.
- [ ] Linux does not leave a second effective application instance after link delivery.
- [ ] Windows support is included only if shared configuration makes it trivial.

## Test Plan

- Add renderer integration tests mocking only Tauri delivery for startup timing, running push, last-URL-wins, Settings behavior, hidden/missing targets, banner lifecycle, focus, and titles.
- Add Rust/configuration tests or build checks for plugin order, capabilities, and static scheme configuration where practical.
- Add a real-desktop macOS/Linux scenario that invokes the registered scheme against isolated state and asserts visible Issue Detail and single-instance behavior where the host permits.
- Run Rust tests, renderer tests, TypeScript build, Ultracite, Tauri build, and available desktop acceptance tests.
title: Open same-Workspace deep links through the desktop application
